title: Asia Centric: How Leverage ETFs Are Shaking Up Asian Markets
summary: The rise of single-stock ETFs, especially CSOP’s 2x leveraged SK Hynix ETF listed in Hong Kong, is reshaping market dynamics. The daily rebalancing of these massive funds is contributing to higher volatility in equity markets, creating new challenges for investors. At the same time, high concentration risk and narrow market leadership are fundamentally changing how investors think about hedging.

title: Google Poised to Lose Two More Senior AI Staffers to Anthropic
summary: Two leading artificial intelligence researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google are planning to leave for rival Anthropic PBC, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a series of high-profile departures that risk undercutting the search giant’s position in AI.Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel, both viewed internally as key contributors to Google’s Gemini AI model, are set to move to the Claude maker, said the people, who spoke on condition of anonymity as the information is not public.

title: NTT Raises $10.4 Billion in Largest Asian Bond Sale This Year
summary: Japanese telecom giant NTT Inc. raised $10.4 billion from a three-currency debt offering Wednesday, the largest global bond sale by an Asian company this year.

title: Bloomberg Businessweek Daily: Poolside Growth (Podcast)

title: Memory Chipmaker SK Hynix's $29 Billion US Listing Seizes on AI Demand
summary: SK Hynix Inc. is seeking 45.45 trillion won ($29.4 billion) in a US listing, tapping investor demand for high-flying memory-chip stocks even after a major selloff shook the group this week.

summary: President Donald Trump’s executive order requiring major changes to the way Americans vote suffered another courtroom defeat, this time in a suit brought by Democratic state attorneys general who disputed the government’s claims of widespread voter fraud.

title: SK Hynix Caps Come-From-Behind AI Success With US Stock Listing
summary: South Korea’s SK Hynix Inc. is making its debut with American investors with a blockbuster US stock listing, capping one of the most dramatic success stories in the AI industry.

title: Trump Nixes Housing Bill Signing, Oil Slides, More
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 6:20

title: Tech Disruptors: SentinelOne CEO on Security Changes, AI Agents
summary: The cybersecurity industry is facing unprecedented challenges as AI models become more capable. On this episode of Tech Disruptors, SentinelOne co-founder and CEO Tomer Weingarten talks about deploying security for AI agents and the evolution of the security operations center (SOC) with LLMs. He joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Mandeep Singh to discuss the impact of Anthropic Mythos, M&A in cybersecurity and the changing nature of attacks as tools become more sophisticated.

title: Plastics Shortages to Keep Food Prices High After War Disruptions
summary: Asian consumers will be facing higher grocery bills for months thanks to costly packaging stemming from the Iran war, which created severe shortages of plastics needed to get food from farms to market.

title: US Stock Futures Surge on Micron’s Strong Forecast: Markets Wrap
summary: US share futures gained after Micron Technology Inc.’s upbeat outlook reignited confidence in the artificial-intelligence trade, helping fuel a late rally on Wall Street.

title: Oil Holds Drop After Erasing Most Wartime Gains as Supply Swells
summary: Oil was steady following a run of losses that’s erased most of the wartime price gains, with fears of a supply crunch subsiding as more ships transit the Strait of Hormuz after early progress on a peace deal.

title: Women Find Foothold in Family Office Investing Landscape
summary:
Dawn Nordberg, head of integrated client solutions and global family office at Citi Wealth, said projections show that 55% of global wealth will be commanded by women by 2030 as the 'great wealth transfer' continues to trend toward women. 
Nordberg said that she is seeing a shift from women being the beneficiaries of a family to being the principal in a family office.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lula Ally Leaves Senate Leadership Job Amid Ties to Bank Scandal
summary: The Brazilian Senate leader from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s party stepped down from his post after a police investigation uncovered connections to failed lender Banco Master.

title: Builder Shares Rise Despite Trump’s Threat to Kill Housing Bill
summary: Katie Hubbard, president of US capital markets for Walton Global, said that the higher end of the housing market is still doing well as high income clients are still paying to buy and build homes. Hubbard said that despite efforts to make home buying more affordable for the wide public, it is hard for federal efforts to increase housing supply to make an impact. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Says Justice Department Looking Into Gasoline Prices
summary: US President Donald Trump said he has ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, complaining that they aren’t falling fast enough.

title: Bain Capital Nears Deal for Stake in Volkswagen’s Everllence Unit
summary: Bain Capital is nearing a deal to buy a controlling stake in Volkswagen AG’s heavy diesel engine unit and end one of Europe’s most hotly-contested private equity auctions, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Qualcomm Says Data Center Chips to Produce ‘Billions’ in ’27
summary: Bloomberg's Mandeep Singh previews Micron earnings and the larger tech environment as companies at the forefront of AI forecast massive earnings. Singh said he finds similarities between the hold oil companies have on energy prices with how chip companies have a control of AI memory and are unlikely to increase that capacity as they bring in money.   (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Crypto Deal Between Cantor SPAC and Adam Back DAT Gets Delayed
summary: A closely watched vote on a tie-up between a Cantor Fitzgerald-linked company and a digital-asset treasury firm led by Adam Back has been delayed.

title: Micron and Qualcomm Lift Chipmakers Amid Rapid AI Growth
summary: Shares of semiconductor stocks are soaring in extended trading on Wednesday, following a strong financial report from Micron Technology Inc. that underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.

title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
summary: AI人材流出続く、米国上場を申請、首位返り咲き視野、「青天井」シナリオ、供給過剰の兆し

title: Basis Trade Fuels Hedge Funds’ US Bond Exposure: Fed Report
summary: Hedge funds’ growing exposure to Treasuries is primarily due to the revival of the cash-futures basis trade, according to a Federal Reserve economist.

title: Big Banks Pass Fed Stress Test, Quickly Boost Payouts
summary: All of the biggest US banks cleared the Federal Reserve’s annual stress test, immediately allowing lenders to boost buybacks and dividends.

title: Redfin Chief Economist Sees Structural Issues in Housing
summary: Daryl Fairweather, Chief Economist at Redfin, discusses the bipartisan Road to Housing Act, which President Trump has opposed despite its passage by a veto-proof majority. The bill represents a significant federal effort to address the national housing supply shortage by tying federal funding to local governments' plans to increase housing supply and streamline zoning and permitting processes. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hertz Stock Drops a Record 41% on Profit Warning, Funding Plan
summary: Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock plunged the most since its initial public offering five years ago after the car renter warned of pressure on earnings and plans to issue new debt.

title: Bitcoin Breaks Below $60,000 Again as Biggest Crypto Buyers Balk
summary: Bitcoin’s slide back below $60,000 is reviving a fear that has largely been absent from crypto for the past two years: what happens when the market’s biggest buyer comes under pressure just as retail traders lose interest.

title: Ex-Tricolor COO Pleads Guilty to Fraud in Company’s Collapse
summary: Former Tricolor Holdings Chief Operating Officer David Goodgame pleaded guilty to fraud charges stemming from the used-car dealer and lender’s collapse last year.

title: Stocks Climb Late After Micron’s Blowout Outlook: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks climbed in late hours on speculation that Micron Technology Inc.’s strong outlook will help reignite confidence in the artificial-intelligence trade that has powered the market rally from war-driven lows.

title: Bitcoin’s Retreating Retail Army Exposes Fresh Fragility
summary: Bitcoin spent years trying to shed its reputation as a playground for speculative retail traders. Its latest selloff is exposing the tradeoff: Wall Street brought scale and legitimacy, but the retail buyers who once helped absorb sharp declines have largely vanished.

title: Wendy’s Stock Soars 26% as Meme Traders Flock to Burger Chain
summary: The meme-stock crowd is rallying behind another beaten up American icon — Wendy’s Co., the fast-food chain known for the Frosty, Dave’s Triple burger and the Biggie Deal.

title: Alphabet’s Dow Debut Shows Index Headache in Tech-Driven Economy
summary: Alphabet Inc.’s addition to the Dow Jones Industrial Average marks another step in the benchmark’s effort to catch up with a market increasingly defined by Big Tech.

title: Micron Earnings Report Exceeds Expectations | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tesla Holders Bet on SpaceX Merger as Musk’s Real Endgame
summary: Bloomberg's Carmen Arroyo joins Dani Burger and Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." It’s been a challenging year for Tesla shareholders, losing 15% while watching Elon Musk’s SpaceX complete the biggest initial public offering ever and immediately become the most valuable entity in the newly minted trillionaire’s collection. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Breaks Below $4,000 as Multi-Year Rally Grinds to a Halt
summary: Gold fell below $4,000 an ounce for the first time since November, as the prospect of higher interest rates and a resurgent dollar bring bullion’s three-year bull market to a halt.

title: Stock Indexes Give Back Gains as Chip-Stock Dip Buying  Fades
summary: US stocks declined for a third straight day as the pullback from chipmakers and other high-flying tech stocks overshadowed speculation that lower oil prices may ease pressure on the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates.

title: Investment Committee: 2026 Outlook for SPACS
summary:
Christine McNerney, portfolio manager at Periscope Capital, Anna Pinedo, global head, capital markets group at Mayer Brown, and Betsy Cohen, chairman and co-founder of Cohen Circle, join Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals."
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Apollo Caps Private Credit Fund After 17% Request to Exit
summary: Bloomberg's Olivia Fishlow joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Apollo is once again limiting withdrawal requests from its largest non-traded private credit fund for retail investors, as broader concerns about the asset class persist. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Novo Nordisk US EVP on Wegovy’s Franchise ‘Halo Effect'
summary: Jamey Millar, Novo Nordisk's Executive Vice President of US Operations, discussed the company's preparations for the upcoming CMS bridge program, which will impact Medicare beneficiaries' access to obesity treatments. Millar highlighted uncertainty about whether patient volume will surge immediately or gradually after the program's July 1 rollout, noting ongoing efforts to educate healthcare providers and pharmacists on patient eligibility. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
